---
schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: 68f0c7f5d48b88aa418f2327288eba8df3e6e730
findings:
  - { id: judged-dcj-1, kind: judged, text: "Story dc-3 redefines the deleted signal as LOCAL-ONLY (branch absent under refs/heads/design/*), but parent dc-4 binds reclamation to 'the ratified gc signals', and the only ratified deleted signal (verdi-store-layout) is git fetch --prune then neither-local-nor-remote. The story's no-edge justification addresses only verdi-store-layout (component spec, no declarable target), yet parent dc-4 IS a feature-decision fragment — a valid exempts-edge target under 02 §Link taxonomy — and dc-3 declares no edge against it and never discusses parent dc-4 at all. A locally-deleted branch whose remote-tracking ref survives is reclaim-eligible under the story's signal but kept under the ratified one, so the divergence is behavioral, not cosmetic. Parent dc-5's cut-from-local-branches-only rule lends the story's reasoning support, but that mitigation belongs in a declared exempts edge on parent dc-4, not in undeclared prose. (confidence 0.55)", target_ref: spec/workbench-directory }
  - { id: judged-dcj-2, kind: judged, text: "Story dc-4 adds a keep-reason parent dc-4 does not ratify: a worktree whose dc-2 lockfile is held by a live process is skipped ('kept: in use by pid N'). Parent dc-4 enumerates exactly one keep exception to signal-driven reclamation (uncommitted changes, disclosed and kept). The story argues no exempts edge is needed because the lock hold is bounded to a single git invocation and the skip is a transient race window — a reasonable materiality argument, but it is exactly the kind of judgment an exempts edge against parent dc-4 (an available fragment target) exists to record; the story acknowledges the parent fragment by name and still declares no edge. (confidence 0.30)", target_ref: spec/workbench-directory }
  - { id: judged-dcj-3, kind: judged, text: "Story dc-3 opts managed worktrees out of retention_days ('reclaim-eligible the instant its signal fires, no grace period') while verdi-store-layout's ratified config comment describes retention_days as the 'gc horizon for merged/deleted refs' — wording plausibly binding all merged/deleted-ref reclamation. The story discloses the ambiguity and its choice, and its structural argument (component specs carry no decision objects, so no exempts target exists) tracks 02 §Kind registry; flagged low-confidence because prose disclosure is the contract's only available discipline here, but the divergence from ratified prose itself remains undeclared in any machine-readable edge. (confidence 0.25)", target_ref: spec/verdi-store-layout }

# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-dcj-1** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: Story dc-3 redefines the deleted signal as LOCAL-ONLY (branch absent under refs/heads/design/*), but parent dc-4 binds reclamation to 'the ratified gc signals', and the only ratified deleted signal (verdi-store-layout) is git fetch --prune then neither-local-nor-remote. The story's no-edge justification addresses only verdi-store-layout (component spec, no declarable target), yet parent dc-4 IS a feature-decision fragment — a valid exempts-edge target under 02 §Link taxonomy — and dc-3 declares no edge against it and never discusses parent dc-4 at all. A locally-deleted branch whose remote-tracking ref survives is reclaim-eligible under the story's signal but kept under the ratified one, so the divergence is behavioral, not cosmetic. Parent dc-5's cut-from-local-branches-only rule lends the story's reasoning support, but that mitigation belongs in a declared exempts edge on parent dc-4, not in undeclared prose. (confidence 0.55)
- **judged-dcj-2** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: Story dc-4 adds a keep-reason parent dc-4 does not ratify: a worktree whose dc-2 lockfile is held by a live process is skipped ('kept: in use by pid N'). Parent dc-4 enumerates exactly one keep exception to signal-driven reclamation (uncommitted changes, disclosed and kept). The story argues no exempts edge is needed because the lock hold is bounded to a single git invocation and the skip is a transient race window — a reasonable materiality argument, but it is exactly the kind of judgment an exempts edge against parent dc-4 (an available fragment target) exists to record; the story acknowledges the parent fragment by name and still declares no edge. (confidence 0.30)
- **judged-dcj-3** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: Story dc-3 opts managed worktrees out of retention_days ('reclaim-eligible the instant its signal fires, no grace period') while verdi-store-layout's ratified config comment describes retention_days as the 'gc horizon for merged/deleted refs' — wording plausibly binding all merged/deleted-ref reclamation. The story discloses the ambiguity and its choice, and its structural argument (component specs carry no decision objects, so no exempts target exists) tracks 02 §Kind registry; flagged low-confidence because prose disclosure is the contract's only available discipline here, but the divergence from ratified prose itself remains undeclared in any machine-readable edge. (confidence 0.25)
